name: Harness Artifact Registry
license: Open-core: the registry engine ships as Apache-2.0 Harness Open Source (formerly Gitness); the managed/enterprise Harness Artifact Registry is proprietary commercial. (Harness Open Source also has a separate Enterprise edition under a non-commercial license, but the core registry engine is Apache-2.0.)
cost: No public per-GB list price. The managed Harness Artifact Registry is sold inside the Harness platform's paid Essentials and Enterprise plans (both Contact Sales / custom pricing); the product page advertises "storage-based pricing with no egress fees." The free platform tier provides the open-source starter platform rather than the managed AR. The self-hostable Harness Open Source edition (same registry engine) is free under Apache-2.0.
perf_notes: No published throughput/latency benchmarks for HAR. The open-source engine (Gitness) is marketed as "ultra lightweight," up in ~30s and runnable on a $4/mo VM. Content is content-addressed by SHA-256 digest (artifact/version/digest model).
scale_notes: SaaS offers multi-region replication (six regions) and disaster recovery; RBAC, quotas. Self-managed edition scales horizontally by externalizing blobs to S3-compatible object storage (vs default local filesystem) and using external Postgres instead of embedded SQLite. HA is a SaaS/self-managed-enterprise property, not a single-binary one.
summary: Harness's AI-native universal artifact registry (Docker, Python/PyPI, npm, Maven, Go, NuGet, Conda, Hugging Face and ~18 formats) with a built-in dependency firewall, Trivy scanning and quarantine, offered as SaaS and self-managed and built on the Apache-2.0 Harness Open Source (Gitness) registry engine.
extras: Dependency Firewall: evaluates packages and their transitive dependencies at ingest and blocks known CVEs, license violations, severity thresholds or untrusted sources before caching
Quarantine + remediation: auto-isolates artifacts failing vuln/compliance checks (including on Warning verdict) until admin release; temporary-access approval workflow with expiring exemptions
Scanning powered by Aqua Trivy with integrations across 40+ security tools feeding OPA policy evaluation; May 2026 added OSS Risk Level and Malicious Package policy templates
AI-native semantic search and an MCP Server for real-time artifact lineage/insights (no PyPI-style programmatic search API)
First-class AI/ML artifacts: Hugging Face proxying, models/datasets/prompts/agents, entitlement tokens for model sharing
~18 formats incl. Docker/Helm-OCI, Maven, Gradle, SBT, Go, Cargo, NuGet, Swift, Dart, PHP Composer, RPM, Conda, Hugging Face, Generic/Raw File; Alpine/RubyGems/Conan announced as coming soon
Webhooks for artifact create/delete events across formats (incl. Python/PyPI) and upstream proxy registries
Custom upstream-proxy registry suffix for non-standard PyPI endpoints (Artifactory/Nexus/self-hosted mirrors not under /simple/); on-demand caching of public PyPI with no full bulk mirror
Apr 2026 added soft delete with a recovery window plus bulk wildcard deletion and an Audit Dashboard tracking every artifact upload and download

Python registries serve a PEP 503 /simple endpoint at pkg.harness.io and, being a standard Simple API behind basic-auth identity tokens, work with any standards client: pip, uv, Poetry, PDM and Pipenv install from it and twine, uv, Poetry, Flit and Hatch publish to its legacy upload API. No documented JSON Simple API (PEP 691), PEP 658 wheel METADATA, PEP 700, PEP 740 attestations or PyPI trusted publishing (OIDC).

SBOM generation and Cosign/Sigstore artifact signing are provided by the separate, tightly integrated Harness Software Supply Chain Assurance (SCS) module rather than the registry engine itself; the AR dependency firewall natively handles Trivy vuln/malware/license scanning and quarantine

Open-core: the identical registry engine is available free as Apache-2.0 Harness Open Source (Gitness), single Go binary, Docker image harness/harness, embedded SQLite default (GITNESS_DATABASE_DRIVER=postgres for external Postgres), filesystem default or S3-compatible storage (GITNESS_REGISTRY_S3_*)
Native integration with the broader Harness platform (CI/CD, SCM/Code, Policy-as-Code/OPA, SSO/SCIM, audit, SEI metrics); Self-Managed Enterprise Edition exposes Prometheus /metrics for monitoring
